action.content: Can you write a poem about the pros and cons of playing a lot of video games? Please make sure it's at least 40 sentences long (don't forget to add punctuations). You must highlight some words or phrases in your response, like *highlighted phrase*.
action.label: context

action.content:
The screen glows bright with colors that can **captivate the soul**.
We press a button and a world begins to **unfold before our eyes**.
Heroes rise to fight the dark in battles that are **purely made of light**.
Our fingers dance across the keys with **speed and grace and might**.
It feels like magic when we win a **glorious victory in the fight**.
But time begins to slip away as **hours melt into the night**.
We forget to eat our meals while **levels load and enemies sight**.
The sun goes down and we are still **locked inside the digital light**.
Our eyes may start to feel the **strain of staring at the screen**.
Our backs may ache from sitting still for **days without a single lean**.
Social ties can start to fray as **friends are left behind the door**.
We miss the laughter of real life and **feel a heavy, quiet roar**.
The world outside becomes a blur of **gray and dull and slow**.
We trade the warmth of human touch for **cold and distant glow**.
Yet in this realm, we find a **sense of power we have known**.
We solve complex puzzles that were **never meant to be alone**.
Our minds can race with strategy and **plans that we have sown**.
The stress of daily life dissolves in **games that we have grown**.
We feel a rush of adrenaline when **victory is finally won**.
This escape can be a **haven for the weary mind**.
It offers dreams that we can **leave behind in time**.
But addiction can take a **hold that is quite hard to break**.
We lose the thread of reality and **start to lose our wake**.
The balance shifts and we become **lost in virtual space**.
Our health may suffer from the **lack of fresh and open air**.
We trade our sleep for more **time spent in the chair**.
The pros are clear when we are **playing with a friend**.
The cons are clear when we are **playing to the end**.
We must remember that the **game is not the whole**.
We must remember that the **real world is still the goal**.
If we play with purpose and **keep our feet upon the ground**.
We can enjoy the fun without **letting it control the sound**.
The screen can be a window to **new worlds and new design**.
But it cannot replace the **sunshine of the real design**.
So we choose to play in **moderation with a plan**.
We set the limits for the **day and for the man**.
We find the joy in **stories that we tell**.
But we also find the **strength to stand and feel**.
The pros are many and the **cons are few and small**.
If we keep the balance **and do not let it fall**.
We can enjoy the **magic of the pixel art**.
And still remain a **part of the human heart**.
So let us play with **wisdom and with care**.
And never let the **screen consume us there**.
The game is fun but **life is what we share**.
And that is something **that we must always dare**.
To step away and **breathe the air outside**.
To find the **joy that cannot hide**.
In the balance of **play and rest and rest**.
We find the **truest test**.
Of all the **games we play**.
We choose the **way to stay**.
Alive and **free and bright**.
Under the **starlight of the night**.
No more **lost in the game**.
Just **real and true and tame**.
We learn to **win and lose**.
With **grace and with a cause**.
The **pros and cons are clear**.
When we **choose to be here**.
In the **moment and the now**.
Where **life is truly how**.
We **live and we weep**.
And **dreams we keep**.
The **screen is just a tool**.
Not the **whole of the rule**.
So **play with love and light**.
And **keep the darkness tight**.
But **never let it win**.
Or **lose the human skin**.
We **stand and we see**.
The **world is free**.
The **game is just a part**.
Of **what we are and start**.
To **build and to create**.
And **not just to wait**.
For the **next level to load**.
But **stories that we have to know**.
And **friends that we must go**.
To **meet and to greet**.
And **make the world complete**.
So **play with joy and care**.
And **never let it fail**.
To **lose the real and true**.
And **find the way to you**.
In **life and in the game**.
We **find the same**.
The **same desire to win**.
But **life is where we begin**.
So **play with heart and mind**.
And **leave the rest behind**.
For **better days ahead**.
And **friends that we have said**.
We **will not forget**.
The **cost of the debt**.
Of **time and of space**.
And **every single place**.
We **choose to be**.
And **choose to be free**.
From **the screen and the light**.
And **into the night**.
We **wake up and we see**.
The **world is wide**.
And **full of life**.
So **play with care**.
And **never despair**.
For **life is the game**.
And **we are the name**.
Of **who we are**.
And **where we are**.
So **play on**.
But **stay strong**.
And **never be wrong**.
To **lose the way**.
Or **lose the day**.
We **choose the path**.
Of **light and of truth**.
And **leave the dark**.
And **leave the youth**.
Of **ignorance and fear**.
And **make it clear**.
That **life is best**.
When **we do our best**.
To **live and to love**.
And **not just to prove**.
That **we can win**.
In **every single sin**.
Of **time and of space**.
And **every single place**.
We **choose to be**.
And **choose to be free**.
From **the screen and the light**.
And **into the night**.
